chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured one-page feeding diary with risk scoring for pediatric feeding disorders, emphasizing measurable data, behavioral restructuring, and intake structure over appetite tonics during a 3-day observation period before follow-up.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a one-page feeding diary for pediatric feeding disorders.
Learn how to use risk behavior scoring to guide counseling intensity.
Identify feeding safety issues such as gagging, choking, or coughing during meals.
Explore strategies to reduce recall bias using photo diaries and structured intake tracking.
key_facts: Diary duration: 3 consecutive days before a 2-week follow-up.
Minimum dataset includes total milk volume (mL), meal/snack timing, portion sizes, and energy-densification methods.
Risk behavior scoring: 0 (none), 1 (sometimes), 2 (most days). High scores prioritize behavioral restructuring.
Photo diary: One 'before and after' photo per meal daily for 3 days to reduce recall bias.
Avoid daily weighing during the diary window; focus on intake structure and behavior.
reference_quote: Use a structured diary to convert 'not eating' into measurable data and make non-adherence visible without blame. (Source: NICE NG75)
